summary.intent: Address three P2 findings from an automated Codex review cycle on PR #704 (RangeCalendar component) in the fogodev/ars-ui Rust UI library, then push and re-trigger review.
summary.outcome: All three edge cases were fixed with regression tests (zero visible_months fallback, stale controlled range attributes during pending selection, invalid external range validation), local gates passed, and a follow-up commit was pushed to the PR branch.

repo: Codex signals an in-progress review via an 'eyes' reaction on the PR issue itself, not on the @codex review comment — the terminal approval signal is a thumbs-up reaction on the issue.
The project uses `cargo xtask spec validate` as a dedicated spec conformance gate separate from unit tests.
RangeCalendar has a proptest suite invoked via `cargo test -p ars-components --tests -- proptest_range_calendar` in addition to unit and spec conformance tests.
Disabled calendars should still accept externally supplied values for display purposes; disabled blocks interaction, not value display.
The local skill at `.agents/skills/waiting-for-codex-review/SKILL.md` documents the expected Codex reaction protocol.

code.finding: visible_months prop normalization happens at init/sync time; zero value was not being clamped to the two-month default before this fix.
Controlled range prop sync path did not clear rendered range attributes while a pending anchor was active, causing stale confirmed-range UI state.
External range values entered Bindable storage without validation; invalid ranges (e.g. end before start, unavailable dates) needed to be rejected at the sync boundary.

workflow: Write failing tests before touching implementation to confirm the bug is exposed, then fix.
Run `cargo fmt --check` and `cargo clippy` before committing; rustfmt and Clippy often require a mechanical cleanup pass after implementation changes.
Run `cargo llvm-cov test --lib --text` scoped to the changed module as a final pre-commit coverage evidence step.
When CI checks are slow to appear after a push, inspect specific run job IDs via `gh api repos/.../actions/runs/<id>/jobs` rather than waiting blindly on `gh pr checks`.

friction: Codex review status was misread initially by watching only the @codex review comment reactions instead of the PR issue-level reactions, requiring a skill file lookup to clarify the correct signal.
CI checks took multiple 180-240 second polling cycles to progress, with several jobs (Coverage, Release Verification, Dioxus feature-flag) in the long tail requiring extended waits.
The new Actions run was not immediately attached to the pushed commit, requiring an extra sleep before `gh pr checks` reflected the correct run.
Clippy and rustfmt both required a separate mechanical fix pass after the initial implementation, adding an extra test-fix-fmt-check cycle.
open_items: A new Codex review cycle was triggered at the end of the session but had not yet returned a result — approval or additional findings are pending.
Coverage and Release Verification CI jobs were still running when the session ended; their results are not confirmed green.
